title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in representative frame-time and capacity evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Frame Budget
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 9272 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-performance-regression-qxoed5" that uses case 3655 to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Performance Regression.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Performance Regression into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Performance Regression, keep Performance Regression connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-performance-regression-qxoed5
# Competency: asteroids-performance-regression.
# Case 3655: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_performance_regression_qxoed5(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_qxoed5 = "qxoed5-5deoxq-3655"
    assert evidence_variant_qxoed5
    evidence_axes_qxoed5 = (
        "playerqxoed5",
        "seed3655",
        "input5deoxq",
        "frameqxoed53655",
        "state3655qxoed5",
        "playtest5deoxq3655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_qxoed5)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Performance Regression structural check is only one gate. representative frame-time and capacity evidence must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
